title: huanuca spathulata
intro: Huanuca spathulata, also known as Huánuco spathulata, is a plant species native to the Andean region in South America, specifically in Peru, belonging to the Rutaceae family and is a type of citrus. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which contain limonene, a compound that has shown antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Huanuca spathulata include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Huanuca spathulata are often made into teas, infusions, or essential oils, which are used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
